proof:
have h₁ : (n % 17) = 5 := by
    have : n % 17 = 0 ∨ n % 17 = 1 ∨ n % 17 = 2 ∨ n % 17 = 3 ∨ n % 17 = 4 ∨ n % 17 = 5 ∨ n % 17 = 6 ∨ n % 17 = 7 ∨ n % 17 = 8 ∨ n % 17 = 9 ∨ n % 17 = 10 ∨ n % 17 = 11 ∨ n % 17 = 12 ∨ n % 17 = 13 ∨ n % 17 = 14 ∨ n % 17 = 15 ∨ n % 17 = 16 := by omega
    rcases this with (h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂) <;>
      simp [h₂, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod] at h₀ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;> (try contradiction) <;> (try norm_num at h₀ ⊢ <;> omega)
  exact h₁
failed_attempts:
have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * n) % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by
      have h₄ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod]
      rw [h₄] at h₂
      exact h₂
    have h₄ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by decide)
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₃ ⊢ <;> omega
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * n) % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
      simp [Nat.mul_mod]
    rw [h₃] at h₂
    have h₄ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := h₂
    have h₅ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt _ (by norm_num)
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₄ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₆ : False := by omega
          contradiction
        }) <;>
      (try omega)
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * n) % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
      have h₄ : 5 * n = 5 * (n % 17 + 17 * (n / 17)) := by
        have h₅ : n = n % 17 + 17 * (n / 17) := by
          have h₆ := Nat.div_add_mod n 17
          linarith
        rw [h₅]
        <;> ring
      rw [h₄]
      simp [Nat.add_mod, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mod_mod, Nat.mod_eq_of_lt]
      <;> norm_num <;> ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod]
      <;> omega
    have h₄ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by
      omega
    have h₅ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      have : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by norm_num)
      interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₄ ⊢ <;> omega
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

have h₁ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by
    have h₁₁ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
      have h₁₂ : 5 * n % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
      exact h₁₂
    rw [h₁₁] at h₀
    exact h₀
  
  have h₂ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₃ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by norm_num)
    have h₄ : n % 17 ≥ 0 := by omega
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₁ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;> (try contradiction)
  
  exact h₂

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by
      have h₃ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod]
      rw [h₃] at h₀
      exact h₀
    have h₃ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by norm_num)
    -- Check each possible value of n % 17 to find that only 5 works
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₂ ⊢ <;> try contradiction
  exact h₁

have h₁ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by sorry
  have h₂ : n % 17 = 5 := by sorry
  sorry

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * n) % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 = 8 := by
      have h₄ : (5 * n) % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod]
        <;> omega
      rw [h₄] at h₂
      exact h₂
    have h₄ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      have h₅ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by norm_num)
      interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₃ ⊢ <;> omega
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : 5 * (n % 17) % 17 = 8 := by
      have h₃ : 5 * n % 17 = 8 := h₀
      have h₄ : 5 * n % 17 = (5 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mod_mod]
      rw [h₄] at h₃
      exact h₃
    -- Now we know that 5 * (n % 17) ≡ 8 mod 17.
    -- We can solve for n % 17 by trying all possible values.
    have h₅ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt n (by decide)
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₂ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;> (try contradiction)
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : 5 * (n % 17) % 17 = 8 := by
      have h₃ : 5 * (n % 17) % 17 = (5 * n) % 17 := by
        simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mod_mod]
      rw [h₃] at *
      exact h₀
    have h₄ : n % 17 < 17 := Nat.mod_lt _ (by norm_num)
    interval_cases n % 17 <;> norm_num at h₂ ⊢ <;>
      (try omega) <;> (try contradiction)
  exact h₁
successful_attempts:
have h₁ : (n % 17) = 5 := by
    have : n % 17 = 0 ∨ n % 17 = 1 ∨ n % 17 = 2 ∨ n % 17 = 3 ∨ n % 17 = 4 ∨ n % 17 = 5 ∨ n % 17 = 6 ∨ n % 17 = 7 ∨ n % 17 = 8 ∨ n % 17 = 9 ∨ n % 17 = 10 ∨ n % 17 = 11 ∨ n % 17 = 12 ∨ n % 17 = 13 ∨ n % 17 = 14 ∨ n % 17 = 15 ∨ n % 17 = 16 := by omega
    rcases this with (h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂ | h₂) <;>

  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : (5 * n) % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      -- We need to find the modular inverse of 5 modulo 17.
      -- The inverse of 5 modulo 17 is 7 because 5 * 7 ≡ 1 mod 17.
      -- Therefore, multiplying both sides of 5n ≡ 8 mod 17 by 7 gives n ≡ 56 ≡ 5 mod 17.
      -- We can check all possible residues of n modulo 17 to find the solution.
      have h₄ : n % 17 = 0 ∨ n % 17 = 1 ∨ n % 17 = 2 ∨ n % 17 = 3 ∨ n % 17 = 4 ∨ n % 17 = 5 ∨ n % 17 = 6 ∨ n % 17 = 7 ∨ n % 17 = 8 ∨ n % 17 = 9 ∨ n % 17 = 10 ∨ n % 17 = 11 ∨ n % 17 = 12 ∨ n % 17 = 13 ∨ n % 17 = 14 ∨ n % 17 = 15 ∨ n % 17 = 16 := by
        omega
      rcases h₄ with (h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try {
          simp [h₄, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod] at h₂ ⊢
          <;> omega
        })
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    have h₂ : 5 * n % 17 = 8 := h₀
    -- We use the fact that 5 * 7 ≡ 1 mod 17 to find the inverse of 5 modulo 17
    -- 7 is the inverse of 5 modulo 17 because 5 * 7 = 35 ≡ 1 mod 17
    -- Multiply both sides of the congruence by 7
    have h₃ : (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 = (7 * 8) % 17 := by
      calc
        (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 = (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 := rfl
        _ = (7 * (5 * n) % 17) := rfl
        _ = (7 * (5 * n % 17)) % 17 := by
          simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod]
        _ = (7 * 8) % 17 := by rw [h₂]
        _ = (7 * 8) % 17 := rfl
    -- Simplify 7 * (5 * n) to 35 * n ≡ 1 * n mod 17
    have h₄ : (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 = (35 * n) % 17 := by
      ring_nf at h₃ ⊢
      <;> omega
    -- Simplify 35 ≡ 1 mod 17, so 35 * n ≡ n mod 17
    have h₅ : (35 * n) % 17 = n % 17 := by
      have h₅₁ : 35 % 17 = 1 := by norm_num
      have h₅₂ : (35 * n) % 17 = (1 * n) % 17 := by
        calc
          (35 * n) % 17 = ((35 % 17) * (n % 17)) % 17 := by
            simp [Nat.mul_mod]
          _ = (1 * (n % 17)) % 17 := by rw [h₅₁]
          _ = (1 * n) % 17 := by simp [Nat.mul_mod]
      have h₅₃ : (1 * n) % 17 = n % 17 := by simp [Nat.mul_mod]
      calc
        (35 * n) % 17 = (1 * n) % 17 := by rw [h₅₂]
        _ = n % 17 := by rw [h₅₃]
    -- Simplify 7 * 8 ≡ 56 ≡ 5 mod 17
    have h₆ : (7 * 8) % 17 = 5 := by norm_num
    -- Combine the congruences to get n ≡ 5 mod 17
    have h₇ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      have h₇₁ : (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 = (7 * 8) % 17 := h₃
      have h₇₂ : (7 * (5 * n)) % 17 = (35 * n) % 17 := by rw [h₄]
      have h₇₃ : (35 * n) % 17 = n % 17 := h₅
      have h₇₄ : (7 * 8) % 17 = 5 := h₆
      have h₇₅ : n % 17 = 5 := by
        omega
      exact h₇₅
    exact h₇
  exact h₁

    have h₃ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      -- Use the fact that 5 and 17 are coprime to find the multiplicative inverse of 5 modulo 17
      have h₄ : n % 17 = 0 ∨ n % 17 = 1 ∨ n % 17 = 2 ∨ n % 17 = 3 ∨ n % 17 = 4 ∨ n % 17 = 5 ∨ n % 17 = 6 ∨ n % 17 = 7 ∨ n % 17 = 8 ∨ n % 17 = 9 ∨ n % 17 = 10 ∨ n % 17 = 11 ∨ n % 17 = 12 ∨ n % 17 = 13 ∨ n % 17 = 14 ∨ n % 17 = 15 ∨ n % 17 = 16 := by
        omega
      rcases h₄ with (h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄ | h₄) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try {
          simp [h₄, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod] at h₂ ⊢ <;>

        })
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
    -- We know that 5 * n ≡ 8 mod 17. We need to find n mod 17.
    -- First, find the multiplicative inverse of 5 modulo 17.
    -- The inverse of 5 modulo 17 is 7 because 5 * 7 = 35 ≡ 1 mod 17.
    -- Multiply both sides of the congruence by 7:
    -- 7 * (5 * n) ≡ 7 * 8 mod 17 => 35 * n ≡ 56 mod 17.
    -- Simplify: 1 * n ≡ 56 mod 17 (since 35 ≡ 1 mod 17).
    -- Further simplify: n ≡ 56 mod 17. Since 56 = 3 * 17 + 5, we have n ≡ 5 mod 17.
    -- Therefore, n % 17 = 5.
    have h₂ : 5 * n % 17 = 8 := h₀
    have h₃ : n % 17 = 5 := by
      -- Use the fact that 5 and 17 are coprime to find the inverse of 5 modulo 17.
      -- The inverse is 7 because 5 * 7 ≡ 1 mod 17.
      -- Multiply both sides of the congruence by 7 to solve for n.
      -- We can use the `omega` tactic to solve the linear congruence.
      omega
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : n % 17 = 5 := by
